meaning: n.鑻︾棝,杈涜荆,鍒烘縺,灏栧埢
examples: Ginger is the traditional spice for its arom and pungency.
In truth sorrow is not desirable, but taken apart its pungency may appear savoury.
Plain: lacking in the accepted desirable characteristics particularly pungency and quality.
All this may add pungency to the Senate hearings on campaign finance, which resume after Labour Day.
The food does not assault the senses with the typical heat and pungency of traditional Korean dishes.
